name: Aglio e Olio
summary: A classic for people who haven't been grocery shopping in three weeks. Garlic, oil, pasta. Done.

steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

While pasta cooks, thinly slice all the garlic. Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-low. Add garlic and chili flakes. Cook slowly until the garlic turns golden — 5 to 7 minutes. Don't rush this.
Add the drained pasta directly to the pan. Add a splash of pasta water and toss hard to emulsify into a silky sauce. Add more water as needed.
Remove from heat. Add parsley and half the parmesan. Toss again. Serve immediately with remaining parmesan on top.

name: Shakshuka
summary: Eggs poached in spiced tomato sauce. The kind of thing that sounds fancier than it is and tastes better than it sounds.

steps.instruction: Dice the onion. Heat olive oil in a wide, lidded pan over medium heat. Cook the onion until soft and starting to colour, about 8 minutes.
Add sliced garlic, cumin, and paprika. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tomatoes. Crush them with a spoon, season generously, and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Make 4 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each. Cover and cook for 5 to 7 minutes — whites set, yolks still runny. Crumble feta over the top and serve straight from the pan.

name: Fried Rice
summary: The correct use of yesterday's leftover rice. Better than ordering in, faster than boiling water.

steps.instruction: Get your wok or largest pan screaming hot. Add a neutral oil and let it smoke. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Push everything to the side. Scramble the eggs in the empty space until just set, then break them up and mix into the garlic.
Add the cold rice. Press it into the pan and leave it alone for 1 minute to get crispy on the bottom. Then stir and repeat.
Add frozen peas, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Toss everything together over high heat for 2 minutes. Serve topped with sliced spring onions.

name: Spiced Chickpea Soup
summary: A can of chickpeas, some pantry spices, and 30 minutes. Genuinely warming. No apologies needed.

steps.instruction: Dice the onion and cook in olive oil over medium heat until soft, about 7 minutes. Add sliced garlic and cumin, stir for 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes and a can's worth of water. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the drained chickpeas. Use a potato masher or the back of a spoon to crush about a third of them — this thickens the soup without blending it.
Stir in the spinach until wilted. Squeeze in the lemon juice, adjust seasoning, and serve.

name: Black Bean Tacos
summary: Fast, loud, satisfying. Works on a Tuesday night when you've got 20 minutes and low expectations.

steps.instruction: Drain and rinse the beans. Add to a pan with cumin, a pinch of salt, and a splash of water. Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, mashing slightly.
Dice the red onion small. Slice the avocado. Roughly chop the coriander. Squeeze lime over the avocado to stop it going brown.
Warm the tortillas directly over a gas flame or in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side.
Layer beans, avocado, red onion, and coriander in each tortilla. Squeeze more lime. Eat immediately.

name: Lemon Chicken Thighs
summary: Bone-in, crispy-skinned, properly lemony. The kind of thing you make when you want to feel like you've got it together.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C. Pat the chicken dry — this is the most important step for crispy skin.
Season the thighs aggressively on both sides. Heat olive oil in an ovenproof pan until almost smoking. Lay thighs skin-side down. Don't touch them for 8 minutes.
Flip, add butter, crushed garlic cloves, thyme, and sliced lemon. Baste the chicken with the butter as it melts.
Transfer the pan to the oven. Roast for 25 minutes until cooked through and the skin is deep golden. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Mushroom Risotto
summary: Requires attention but rewards it. Creamy, earthy, the kind of thing you eat in silence because it deserves it.

steps.instruction: Keep your stock warm in a separate pan. Dice the onion finely. Slice the mushrooms. Grate the parmesan.
Melt half the butter in a wide, heavy pan. Cook the onion over low heat until completely soft, about 10 minutes. Add garlic for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms, turn up the heat, and cook until they release their liquid and it evaporates. This takes patience — don't rush.
Add the rice and stir for 2 minutes until the edges turn translucent. Pour in the wine and stir until absorbed.
Add hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly and adding more only when each addition is absorbed. This takes 18 to 20 minutes. The rice should be creamy and just cooked.
Remove from heat. Add remaining butter and parmesan. Stir vigorously for 1 minute. Season and serve immediately.

name: Peanut Noodles
summary: Cold noodles, big peanut sauce, ready in 15 minutes. Good at room temperature. Good cold. Good always.

steps.instruction: Cook noodles according to packet instructions. Drain and rinse under cold water. Toss with a little sesame oil so they don't stick.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, sriracha, sesame oil, grated garlic, and lime juice. Add warm water a tablespoon at a time until you have a pourable sauce.
Julienne or thinly slice the cucumber. Toss noodles with the sauce until well coated.
Serve topped with cucumber. Add more sriracha if you mean it.
